params.text:
Notifiable safety incidents

The formal part of the statutory duty is triggered by what Regulation 20 calls a notifiable safety incident. This is an unintended or unexpected incident that happened during a person’s care and that, in the reasonable opinion of a healthcare professional, appears to have resulted in — or could still result in — a significant degree of harm.

  • Unintended or unexpected: it was not a planned or normal part of care.
  • Linked to the care: it happened during the provision of the care service.
  • Reaches the harm threshold: a healthcare professional judges the harm to be significant.

The threshold of harm

Not every mistake is a notifiable safety incident in the legal sense — but every mistake still calls for openness and honesty. The formal duty is triggered when the harm reaches a defined threshold. For a care provider, this includes an incident that appears to have caused death, severe or moderate harm, or prolonged pain or psychological harm — where ‘prolonged’ means lasting, or likely to last, for a continuous period of at least 28 days.

You are not expected to make this legal judgement yourself, and you should never try to decide alone whether an incident ‘counts’. Your task is to report clearly and promptly so that your manager and the relevant healthcare professionals can assess the harm against the threshold and take the right formal steps.

params.taskDescription: Drag each word into the correct gap.
params.textField: A notifiable safety incident is *unintended* or *unexpected*, and the harm it causes reaches a threshold such as *moderate*, *severe* or *prolonged* harm.
